Patch Set 8:

Jian,

There is already an open ticket in JIRA for the ll_encode_fh LBUG (https://jira.hpdd.intel.com/browse/LU-3717)

Patch Set 9:

Build Started http://build.whamcloud.com/job/lustre-reviews/18982/

Patch Set 9: Fails

Build Failed 
 
http://build.whamcloud.com/job/lustre-reviews/18982/ : FAILURE

Patch Set 9:

After changing lustre-core.m4 to do a kernel-level check to see if the syscalls exist, the build now fails on nodes where the syscalls exist in kernel but are not defined in userspace headers.

Patch Set 9: I would prefer that you didn't submit this

Maybe we can change to use syscall(SYS_name_to_handle_at, ...) and syscall(SYS_open_by_handle_at, ...) in check_fhandle_syscalls.c?

Or change lustre-core.m4 back to check the name_to_handle_at() and open_by_handle_at() support in glibc?

Patch Set 9:

Yes, I will upload a new patchset with the syscall change. That way we will not need the FC-18 client check in sanity.sh as well.
Uploaded patch set 10.

(11 comments)

Patch Set 22:

(1 comment)

I am concerned that we are landign a test that is compiled out now, but once it starts to run, our testing might be ruined with very little warning.

Patch Set 22:

Swapnil, have you ever tested this on a kernel/glibc that has the open_by_handle_at() syscall?

Patch Set 22:

Is maloo run against SLES11 SP3 servers? By default the SP3 kernels enable fhandles but the functions are not supported in glibc. So this test is run for real on SP3 boxes using the wrappers.I also tested it on a RHE6 system with a mainline kernel.The only one I haven't tested is a fc19+ box which would support both.

Patch Set 22:

Yes, I have tested this on FC-18 clients with kernel: 3.6.10-4.fc18.x86_64 and glibc: 2.16
Change has been successfully cherry-picked as 3f5fc6412d3ae2d3a57f8fdb8a457f35e9d9576e

parents.subject: LU-2524 tests: run sanity test_51ba in test_51b dir

commit.subject: LU-3344 tests: Verify file handle system calls
commit.message:
LU-3344 tests: Verify file handle system calls

New system calls name_to_handle_at() and open_by_handle_at() are added
to Linux kernel 2.6.39. Added test to verify these work correctly with
Lustre.

Signed-off-by: Swapnil Pimpale <spimpale@ddn.com>
Signed-off-by: James Simmons <uja.ornl@gmail.com>
Change-Id: Icbfc9642cd550ac44d379263836782ffbf4a74f4
Reviewed-on: http://review.whamcloud.com/7247
Reviewed-by: Andreas Dilger <andreas.dilger@intel.com>
Tested-by: Jenkins
Reviewed-by: Jian Yu <jian.yu@intel.com>
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: John L. Hammond <john.hammond@intel.com>
